title: 351. Which Article of the Indian Constitution guarantees Equality Before Law?
hint: It is the first article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 14 guarantees equality before law and equal protection of laws.

title: 352. Which Article abolishes Untouchability?
hint: It is part of the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 17 abolishes untouchability and forbids its practice in any form.

title: 353. Which Article guarantees protection of life and personal liberty?
hint: It became very important after the Maneka Gandhi case.
summary: Article 21 guarantees protection of life and personal liberty.

title: 354. Which Article is known as the 'Heart and Soul' of the Constitution according to Dr. B.R. Ambedkar?
hint: It provides the Right to Constitutional Remedies.
summary: Article 32 allows citizens to directly approach the Supreme Court for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

title: 355. Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex, or place of birth?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 14.
summary: Article 15 prohibits discrimination by the State on specified grounds.

title: 356. Which Article guarantees equality of opportunity in public employment?
hint: Closely related to government jobs.
summary: Article 16 ensures equality of opportunity in matters of public employment.

title: 357. Which Article abolishes titles except military and academic distinctions?
hint: Prevents the State from conferring hereditary titles.
summary: Article 18 abolishes titles and promotes equality.

title: 358. Which Article provides six freedoms to citizens of India?
hint: Freedom of speech is part of it.
summary: Article 19 grants freedoms including speech, movement and association.

title: 359. Which Article protects against ex-post facto laws, double jeopardy and self-incrimination?
hint: A key safeguard in criminal law.
summary: Article 20 provides protection in respect of conviction for offences.

title: 360. Which Article prohibits human trafficking and forced labour?
hint: Includes prohibition of begar.
summary: Article 23 prohibits trafficking in human beings and forced labour.

title: 361. Which Article prohibits employment of children below 14 years in hazardous occupations?
hint: Closely linked to child welfare.
summary: Article 24 prohibits child labour in hazardous industries.

title: 362. Which Article provides free and compulsory education to children aged 6–14 years?
hint: Added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment.
summary: Article 21A makes education a Fundamental Right.

title: 363. Which Article contains Directive Principles of State Policy related to equal justice and free legal aid?
hint: Inserted by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment.
summary: Article 39A promotes equal justice and free legal aid.

title: 364. Which Article directs the State to organize village panchayats?
hint: A Gandhian principle.
summary: Article 40 directs the State to organize village panchayats.

title: 365. Which Article deals with Uniform Civil Code?
hint: Frequently discussed in current affairs.
summary: Article 44 directs the State to secure a Uniform Civil Code.

title: 366. Which Article promotes educational and economic interests of SCs, STs and weaker sections?
hint: Part of DPSP.
summary: Article 46 directs the State to promote the interests of weaker sections.

title: 367. Which Article directs the State to improve public health and prohibit intoxicating drinks?
hint: Often cited in prohibition policies.
summary: Article 47 deals with nutrition, public health and prohibition.

title: 368. Which Article directs the State to organize agriculture and animal husbandry on modern lines?
hint: Also associated with cow protection.
summary: Article 48 focuses on agriculture, animal husbandry and preservation of cattle.

title: 369. Which Article directs separation of judiciary from executive?
hint: Important for judicial independence.
summary: Article 50 provides for separation of judiciary from executive.

title: 370. Which Article contains Fundamental Duties?
hint: Added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment.
summary: Article 51A enumerates the Fundamental Duties of citizens.

title: 371. Which Article establishes the office of the President of India?
hint: The first article under Union Executive.
summary: Article 52 states that there shall be a President of India.

title: 372. Which Article vests the executive power of the Union in the President?
hint: Immediately follows Article 52.
summary: Article 53 vests executive power of the Union in the President.

title: 373. Which Article deals with the election of the President?
hint: Electoral College is mentioned here.
summary: Article 54 provides for election of the President.

title: 374. Which Article establishes the office of the Vice-President of India?
hint: The number is between 60 and 70.
summary: Article 63 states that there shall be a Vice-President of India.

title: 375. Which Article states that the Vice-President shall be the ex-officio Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
hint: It immediately follows the article creating the office of Vice-President.
summary: Article 64 makes the Vice-President the ex-officio Chairman of the Council of States.

title: 376. Which Article deals with the election of the Vice-President of India?
hint: It comes immediately after the article making the Vice-President Chairman of the Rajya Sabha.
summary: Article 66 provides for the election of the Vice-President by members of both Houses of Parliament.

title: 377. Which Article deals with the Attorney General for India?
hint: It is the last article in the Union Executive chapter before the Council of Ministers.
summary: Article 76 provides for the office of the Attorney General for India.

title: 378. Which Article provides for a Council of Ministers to aid and advise the President?
hint: This article became important after the 42nd and 44th Amendments.
summary: Article 74 provides for the Council of Ministers headed by the Prime Minister.

title: 379. Which Article deals with the appointment of the Prime Minister and other Ministers?
hint: It follows Article 74.
summary: Article 75 deals with appointment, tenure and responsibility of ministers.

title: 380. Which Article states that there shall be a Parliament for the Union consisting of the President and two Houses?
hint: It is the opening article of the Parliament chapter.
summary: Article 79 establishes the Parliament of India.

title: 381. Which Article deals with the composition of the Rajya Sabha?
hint: Maximum strength of 250 members is linked to this article.
summary: Article 80 provides for the composition of the Council of States.

title: 382. Which Article deals with the composition of the Lok Sabha?
hint: It immediately follows the Rajya Sabha composition article.
summary: Article 81 provides for the composition of the House of the People.

title: 383. Which Article deals with the duration of Houses of Parliament?
hint: Lok Sabha's normal tenure of five years comes from this article.
summary: Article 83 specifies the duration of Rajya Sabha and Lok Sabha.

title: 384. Which Article empowers the President to summon and prorogue Parliament?
hint: It comes after the article dealing with duration.
summary: Article 85 deals with sessions, prorogation and dissolution of Parliament.

title: 385. Which Article provides special address by the President at the commencement of the first session after each general election?
hint: The President addresses both Houses assembled together.
summary: Article 87 provides for the President's special address.

title: 386. Which Article deals with the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of the Rajya Sabha?
hint: The Vice-President is connected with this provision.
summary: Article 89 provides for the Chairman and Deputy Chairman of Rajya Sabha.

title: 387. Which Article deals with the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha?
hint: It mirrors Article 89 for the Lower House.
summary: Article 93 provides for the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of Lok Sabha.

title: 388. Which Article deals with salaries and allowances of the Chairman and Speaker?
hint: It is near Articles 97 and 98.
summary: Article 97 deals with salaries and allowances of presiding officers of Parliament.

title: 389. Which Article grants freedom of speech in Parliament subject to constitutional provisions?
hint: Parliamentary privileges begin around this article.
summary: Article 105 deals with powers, privileges and immunities of Parliament and its members.

title: 390. Which Article deals with the introduction and passing of ordinary bills in Parliament?
hint: It starts the legislative procedure chapter.
summary: Article 107 deals with provisions as to introduction and passing of bills.

title: 391. Which Article provides for a Joint Sitting of both Houses of Parliament?
hint: Used to resolve deadlocks between the two Houses.
summary: Article 108 empowers the President to summon a joint sitting.

title: 392. Which Article defines a Money Bill?
hint: The Speaker's certification is crucial under this provision.
summary: Article 110 defines a Money Bill under the Constitution.

title: 393. Which Article deals with the Annual Financial Statement (Union Budget)?
hint: Presented every year in Parliament.
summary: Article 112 provides for the Annual Financial Statement.

title: 394. Which Article establishes the Supreme Court of India?
hint: The Judiciary chapter begins with this article.
summary: Article 124 provides for the establishment and constitution of the Supreme Court.

title: 395. Which Article empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights?
hint: This power makes the Court a protector of rights.
summary: Article 32 empowers the Supreme Court to issue writs.

title: 396. Which Article provides for the Comptroller and Auditor General (CAG) of India?
hint: The constitutional watchdog of public finances.
summary: Article 148 provides for the CAG of India.

title: 397. Which Article provides for the office of the Governor of a State?
hint: It begins the chapter on State Executive.
summary: Article 153 states that there shall be a Governor for each State.

title: 398. Which Article provides for the Election Commission of India?
hint: This article is frequently cited during elections.
summary: Article 324 vests the superintendence, direction and control of elections in the Election Commission.

title: 399. Which Article deals with National Emergency?
hint: It can be proclaimed on grounds of war, external aggression or armed rebellion.
summary: Article 352 provides for proclamation of National Emergency.

title: 400. Which Article provides for President's Rule in a State?
hint: Often referred to as State Emergency.
summary: Article 356 empowers the President to assume functions of a State government under certain circumstances.
